summary: Relaxed midsummer set with sparkling Morning Dew and debut Easy Wind
blurb: The show features a mellow first set that flows into a cozy second half with smooth transitions. Highlights include a sparkling rendition of Morning Dew, the debut of Easy Wind, and an extended The Other One > Dark Star > Cosmic Charlie sequence. Drumming by Mickey and Bill is balanced and supportive throughout.
review:
Seattle’s Aqua Theatre on August 21, 1969 captured one of those late‑summer evenings where the Dead were content to let the music breathe. Jerry’s voice shines on “Doin’ That Rag” while his guitar tone stays crystal clear, and both Mickey Hart and Bill Kreutzmann lay down a smooth, balanced rhythmic foundation that lets the jams unfold naturally. The set opens with a straightforward “Casey Jones” before slipping into the first‑time appearance of “Easy Wind,” a gentle highlight that segues into an ethereal “Morning Dew.” A laid‑back second half features the classic “New Minglewood Blues” and a rare, flowing trio—“The Other One > Dark Star > Cosmic Charlie”—that showcases the group’s improvisational patience. Even the novelty flute‑colored “China Cat Sunflower” adds a quirky touch without raising the energy too high, keeping the overall vibe mellow yet satisfying.

Both available soundboard tapes are good enough to hear every nuance: the Cotsman recording is especially crisp, capturing stage ambience and subtle details like bells and whistles with only faint tape hiss. The Tremblay board is also clear but lacks the extra sparkle of the Cotsman source. For anyone looking to experience a relaxed, beautifully balanced snapshot of the Dead in ’69, the Cotsman SBD is the definitive listen.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: One recording is clearly positive while the other is mixed; overall sentiment leans mildly favorable with consistent praise for Morning Dew, Easy Wind, and smooth drumming, but no strong consensus of legendary status.

best_recording.reason: Crisp soundboard with clear mix, captures bells, whistles and stage ambience; highlights include Morning Dew, Easy Wind, and smooth drumming.

key_highlights: Relaxed, meandering flow typical of late‑summer ’69 shows
Sparkling rendition of “Morning Dew” and debut of “Easy Wind”
Extended “The Other One > Dark Star > Cosmic Charlie” sequence
Flute‑enhanced “China Cat Sunflower” adds a quirky novelty

band_performance.Jerry: His voice is beautiful, especially on “Doin' That Rag,” and his guitar tone is praised for clarity.
band_performance.Mickey: Drumming is balanced and smooth, providing a solid rhythmic foundation throughout the set.
band_performance.Billy: Drumming is balanced and smooth, providing a solid rhythmic foundation throughout the set.
